dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.purpose: Your daily command centre — today's activity, cash position, stock summary, and shortcuts to common tasks.
steps: Step 1: Confirm the correct branch is selected in the top toolbar (hidden if you have only one branch).
Step 2: Review Quick Actions — use these buttons to start a new loan, renewal, part payment, or other common tasks.
Step 3: Check Today's Operations — each card shows today's count only (not lifetime totals):
• New customers (today) — customers registered today in this branch
• New loans (today) — loans with today's loan date
• Interest collected (today) — part payments + interest on closes today (not full redemption cash)
• Partial payments — number of part payments recorded today
• Released loans (today) — loans fully closed today (renewals are not counted)
• Expenses (today) — daily book expense + petty cash entries for today
Step 4: Review Financial Snapshot — cash in hand, opening balance, collections, and monthly profit.
Step 5: Check Inventory & Gold Stock — weights show grams with 2 decimal places.
Step 6: Click any card or alert to jump to the related screen.
dashboard.example: At opening: Today's Operations shows 2 new loans and ₹625 interest collected. Renewals Due shows 5 loans — open Renewals from Quick Actions before taking new pawns.
tips: All figures are for the selected branch and today's business date.
Interest collected does not include principal when a customer redeems a loan.
Expenses include petty cash recorded under Daily book → Petty cash.

customers-list.title: Customers
customers-list.purpose: Search and open customer records before creating or servicing loans.
steps: Type a customer name or mobile number in the search box.
Click a row to open the customer profile and loan history.
Click New Customer to register someone who has never pawned before.
customers-list.example: A customer says their ID is 1000 — search "அமுதா" or scroll the list, open the profile, then click New Loan from their loan history.
tips: Customer ID (business number) is shown on receipts and must be unique.
Deleted customers are hidden from the default list.

customers-new.title: New Customer
customers-new.purpose: Register a customer before their first pawn loan.
steps: Accept the suggested Customer ID or enter the next available number.
Fill name, father/husband name, full address, city, state, PIN, and mobile.
Click Save — you will land on the profile to add KYC documents if needed.
customers-new.example: New customer Lakshmi, mobile 9876543210, address Nadar New Street Usilampatti — save as ID 5821, then go to Loans → New Pawn Loan.
tips: Double-check mobile number — used for SMS reminders when notifications are enabled.
Photo can be added later from the customer detail page.

customers-detail.title: Customer Profile
customers-detail.purpose: View contact details, KYC documents, and complete loan history for one customer.
steps: Review Profile for address and contact numbers.
Open KYC & IDs to verify or upload identity documents.
Use the Loans table to open any receipt or start a new pawn.
customers-detail.example: Customer #1000 has closed receipt #1014788 (2018) and may have newer open loans — use the Loans section to see all receipts without searching the main loan list.
tips: Click Edit to update address or mobile after a customer moves.
Loan links open the full pawn detail including collateral lines.

customers-edit.title: Edit Customer
customers-edit.purpose: Update customer contact or address information.
steps: Change only the fields that need correction.
Save — changes apply to future receipts; past printed receipts are unchanged.
customers-edit.example: Customer changed from 8056371451 to 9123456780 — update mobile here so renewal SMS reaches the right number.
tips: Customer ID cannot be changed after creation — create a new profile only if truly a different person.

loans-list.title: Pawn Loans
loans-list.purpose: Search all pawn receipts by number, customer name, or status.
steps: Search by receipt number (e.g. 92288) or customer name.
Filter by open, closed, gold, or silver as needed.
Click a row for full loan detail, or New Pawn Loan for a fresh pledge.
loans-list.example: Customer brings receipt #92288 — search 92288, confirm name and amount match, then process renewal or part payment from the detail page.
tips: Receipt numbers are unique per branch.
Closed loans remain searchable for audit and reprints.

loans-new.title: New Pawn Loan
loans-new.purpose: Create a pawn against pledged gold or silver items and print a receipt.
steps: Step 1: Search and select the customer (or create a new customer first).
Step 2: Enter a new receipt number — must not already exist in this branch.
Step 3: Set loan date, commodity (Gold/Silver), loan condition, and customer type.
Step 4: Add each collateral item: sub-category, item type, purity, quantity, and net weight (grams).
Step 5: Enter loan amount — amount in words fills automatically.
Step 6: Confirm interest rate and renewal date, then click Save.
Step 7: Open the loan detail page and print the receipt.
Step 8: If QR codes are enabled (Settings → Application), one QR code is created automatically for this receipt.
loans-new.example: Customer #4242 pledges one gold chain (செயின்), 8.500 g, KDM 916, loan ₹23,000, receipt #92289, renewal 12 months from today.
tips: Interest % comes from Masters → Interest declarations for the amount slab.
Add multiple collateral lines for items pledged together on one receipt.
QR code (when enabled) shows customer name, ID, mobile, and receipt number — one per receipt, not per item.

loans-detail.title: Loan Detail
loans-detail.purpose: Full view of one pawn: customer, collateral, payments, renewal chain, bank status, and receipt QR code.
steps: Step 1: Check the receipt header — receipt number, customer name, and status badge.
Step 2: If QR codes are enabled, one QR appears next to the receipt title. Scanning shows: customer name, customer ID, mobile, and receipt number.
Step 3: Review the Loan card — date, amount, interest, commodity, and net weight.
Step 4: Review the Customer card — name, ID, mobile, and address.
Step 5: Check Interest (as of today) for amount due if the loan is still open.
Step 6: Review the Collateral Items table — verify each pledged item and weight.
Step 7: Use the action icons (top right): Print, Renew, Close, or Part payment as needed.
loans-detail.example: Receipt #50002 — QR next to the title shows customer நாகலட்சுமி, ID 1001, mobile 9751843753, receipt 50002. Staff can scan the QR to verify identity at the counter.
tips: One QR code per receipt — not one per collateral line.
Part-payment history appears on this page.
Bank deposit badge shows if the loan is pledged with a bank.
Edit is only available for open loans with no active bank deposit.

loans-edit.title: Edit Loan
loans-edit.purpose: Correct collateral or header fields on an open (unsettled) loan only.
steps: Open the loan from the list — it must be status Open.
Adjust weights, items, or summary text as needed.
Save — do not change receipt number unless your shop policy allows.
loans-edit.example: Chain weighed 8.50 g but scale read 8.05 g — open Edit, fix net weight on the line, save before printing a corrected receipt copy.
tips: Settled loans cannot be edited — contact a supervisor for historical corrections.
Changing loan amount may require manager approval per shop policy.

loans-close.title: Close Loan
loans-close.purpose: Settle a loan when the customer redeems pledged items and pays principal plus interest.
steps: Confirm customer identity and receipt number.
Enter settlement amount and date.
Choose bill settlement options if applicable.
Enter security PIN when prompted, then confirm close.
loans-close.example: Open loan #92288, principal ₹11,000 plus interest ₹1,320 — customer pays ₹12,320, you close with bill, release chain from vault.
tips: Verify items against collateral lines before handing over jewellery.
Closed loans stay in the system for reports and audit.

loans-print.title: Print Receipt
loans-print.purpose: Print-friendly pawn receipt for the customer or shop copy, including QR code when enabled.
steps: Step 1: Open from loan detail → Print icon, or go to /loans/:id/print.
Step 2: Review customer copy and company copy on screen.
Step 3: If QR codes are enabled, one QR appears in the receipt header (top right) — not on each item line.
Step 4: Press Ctrl+P (or the Print button) and choose your printer.
Step 5: Hand the customer copy to the customer; keep the company copy in your files.
loans-print.example: After saving new loan #92289, print two copies — customer copy with QR for easy lookup, shop copy for the file. QR encodes name, ID, mobile, and receipt #92289.
tips: Update branch address under Settings → Branches if receipts show old details.
Tamil item names come from Master Data sub-items.
Enable QR codes under Settings → Application → Loan item QR codes.

part-payments-list.title: Part Payment
part-payments-list.purpose: View loans with interest payments and navigate to record new part payments.
steps: Search by receipt or customer.
Open a loan to see payment history.
Go to Record to accept a new partial interest payment.
part-payments-list.example: Customer on receipt #92288 pays ₹500 interest without renewing — find the loan here, then Record part payment for ₹500 today.
tips: Part payment does not extend renewal date unless you also renew.
Legacy data includes 1,709 part-payment records.

part-payments-record.title: Record Part Payment
part-payments-record.purpose: Accept partial interest payment without closing or renewing the loan.
steps: Find the loan by receipt number.
Enter amount received and payment date.
Save — payment appears on loan detail and reports.
part-payments-record.example: Receipt #92287, customer pays ₹400 toward interest on 15th of the month — record amount ₹400, date today, print acknowledgement if required.
tips: Amount cannot exceed outstanding interest calculated by the system.
Use Renewals if the customer wants a new term and receipt number.

renewals-list.title: Renewal & Defaults
renewals-list.purpose: Loans approaching or past renewal date; manage defaults before auction.
steps: Sort or filter by due date to prioritise today's renewals.
Open Easy Renewal for customers ready to extend the loan.
Flag or track defaults that may proceed to auction notice.
renewals-list.example: 2,107 open loans are past renewal date in legacy data — filter overdue, call customers starting with highest amounts, offer renewal or part payment.
tips: Renewal creates a linked new receipt; old receipt is marked renewed.
Security PIN is required for renewal in most roles.

renewals-record.title: Easy Renewal
renewals-record.purpose: Renew an existing loan with a new receipt, adjusted principal, and interest settlement.
steps: Find the current open loan by receipt number.
Enter new receipt number, new loan amount, and renewal date.
Enter interest discount or top-up if applicable.
Provide security PIN and confirm.
renewals-record.example: Old receipt #92288, principal ₹11,000 — customer renews for another year at ₹11,000, new receipt #92290, interest ₹1,100 collected separately.
tips: New receipt number must be unique in the branch.
Old loan ID is stored in the renewal chain for audit.

bank-loans-list.title: Bank Loan
bank-loans-list.purpose: Track pawn loans deposited with banks for liquidity (re-pledge).
steps: View active bank deposits and settlement status.
Use Record for a single deposit or Batch for multiple loans.
Open a row to see bank name, deposit date, and closing date.
bank-loans-list.example: 18,363 bank deposits in legacy data — filter unsettled to see total amount currently with Tamil Nadu Mercantile Bank or other lenders.
tips: Enable this module under Settings → Application preferences.
Closing date is set when the bank loan is repaid and items return.

bank-loans-record.title: Record Bank Deposit
bank-loans-record.purpose: Register one pawn loan pledged to a bank.
steps: Select the pawn loan by receipt number.
Enter bank name, bank receipt number, deposit amount, and dates.
Save — loan detail shows bank deposit status.
bank-loans-record.example: Receipt #92286 deposited with TMB, bank receipt BR-4521, ₹24,200, deposit date today — record and keep pawn receipt in bank file.
tips: Customer pawn receipt remains in your system; bank holds physical items.
Mark settled when bank loan is cleared and items return.

bank-loans-batch.title: Batch Bank Deposit
bank-loans-batch.purpose: Deposit multiple pawn loans to a bank in one operation.
steps: Select bank and deposit date.
Add multiple loans from the picker or search.
Confirm total amount and save the batch.
bank-loans-batch.example: Select 25 open gold loans totalling ₹4,50,000, deposit to IOB Usilampatti branch on 31 Jan — one batch instead of 25 separate entries.
tips: Use Dashboard Quick Action Bank for faster access.
Verify each receipt is open and not already deposited.

auctions.title: Auction
auctions.purpose: Manage auction notices and sale of unredeemed pledged items.
steps: Identify defaulted loans past legal notice period.
Create auction notice with date and charges.
Record sale amount, surplus refund, or deficit after auction.
auctions.example: Loan overdue 18 months, legal notice sent — schedule auction 30 days out, publish list, after sale record proceeds and any surplus owed to customer.
tips: Enable module in Application preferences.
SMS auction template available under Notifications.

inventory.title: Inventory / Stock Check
inventory.purpose: Search pledged collateral by receipt, customer, weight, and status. Assign locker and location for vault items.
steps: Step 1: Enter search criteria — receipt number, customer name/phone/ID, status, commodity, or weight range.
Step 2: Click Search. Status summary chips show counts: Available, Bank pledged, Released, etc.
Step 3: Click a status chip to filter, or browse the results table.
Step 4: Click Manage on a row to open that receipt's item list.
Step 5: In the manage panel, see all collateral lines. If QR is enabled, one QR for the whole receipt appears at the top.
Step 6: Click Edit on an item to set locker number, location, status (lost/damaged/transferred), or notes.
Step 7: The edit form shows which item you are editing (name and weight). Click Save or Cancel when done.
inventory.example: Customer says receipt #50002 — search 50002, click Manage, confirm Fancy Earring 2.00 g and other lines match vault tags. Set Locker A-12 and Location Vault-1 for each item.
tips: This screen tracks live pawn collateral — not retail sale stock.
Weights display with 2 decimal places (e.g. 8.60 g).
QR scan is not in search — use receipt number or customer details to find loans.
QR on the manage panel is the same one QR for the entire receipt.

accounts.title: Daily Book & Cash
accounts.purpose: Single shop cash register — daily income/expense, note counts at day close, and bank deposits when over limit.
steps: Step 1: Open Accounts → Daily book tab for entries and the auto ledger.
Step 2: Select today's date (or the date you are closing).
Step 3: Review summary cards — Opening, Cash in hand, Collections, Closing.
Step 4: Add manual entries: Income, Expense, Petty cash, or Top-up (cash added to the drawer) with amount and who recorded it.
Step 5: Open Day close tab when ready to end the day.
Step 6: Count each note and coin (₹2000 down to ₹1) — physical total is calculated automatically.
Step 7: Confirm Closing balance (Current) matches your count, then click Close day — the button sits next to the Notes box.
Step 8: If cash exceeds the shop limit (Settings → Application), deposit excess to bank the next day using Deposit cash to bank.
accounts.example: Physical count: 50×₹2000 + 100×₹500 = ₹1,50,000. Closing balance (Current) ₹1,49,875 — variance ₹125 noted. Limit ₹5,00,000 — no bank deposit needed. If count were ₹6,20,000, deposit ₹1,20,000 to TMB next morning.
tips: There is only one cash total — no vault/counter split.
Closing balance (Current) is your counted cash — it carries forward as the next day's opening balance.
Top-up adds cash to the drawer (e.g. an owner cash injection) and counts as cash in, like income.
Loan collections and closes appear in the ledger automatically.
Bank deposit here is shop cash to your bank account — not loan re-pledge.
Set cash limit under Settings → Application preferences (Super Admin).

investments.title: Investments
investments.purpose: Track shop investments and returns (optional module).
steps: Add investment with amount, date, and expected return.
Record maturity or partial withdrawal.
Review list for upcoming maturities.
investments.example: Shop FD ₹5,00,000 at 7% for 1 year — record start date, mark matured when principal returns to vault.
tips: Enable under Application preferences.
Separate from pawn loan principal.

gl.title: General Ledger
steps: Select date range and account heads.
Review debit/credit entries posted from loans and accounts.
Export for CA or audit if needed.
gl.example: Filter March entries — verify interest income credits match part-payment and renewal collections from reports.
tips: Enable under Application preferences.
Posting rules depend on configured chart of accounts.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.purpose: SMS templates and renewal or auction notices.
steps: Edit template text for renewal_due and auction_notice.
Use placeholders: {customer_name}, {loan_id}, {due_date}, {amount}, {branch_name}.
Send or schedule notices from loan workflows where integrated.
notifications.example: Template: "Hi {customer_name}, loan {loan_id} due {due_date}. Amount ₹{amount}. Visit {branch_name}." — sends before renewal date for open loans.
tips: Tamil body (bodyTa) used when customer language is Tamil.
Requires SMS gateway configuration in production.

pay-advances.title: Pay Advances
pay-advances.purpose: Record staff salary advances and recoveries.
steps: Select employee from master list.
Enter advance amount and date.
Record recovery against future salary.
pay-advances.example: Employee Kumar requests ₹5,000 advance — record today, deduct ₹1,000/month for 5 months in recovery entries.
tips: Employees must exist under Masters → Employees.
Link to daily book for cash outflow.

reports.title: Reports
reports.purpose: Loan registers, cash summaries, and operational exports.
steps: Choose report type from the list.
Set date range and branch filter.
Run report and export or print.
reports.example: Generate Open Loans as of today for Main branch — 4,394 open loans, export to Excel for manager review.
tips: Large date ranges may take longer with 54,000+ historical loans.
Default report opens to most-used register.

settings-organization.title: Organization Profile
settings-organization.purpose: Shop name, proprietor, company logo, and details printed on receipts.
steps: Upload a company logo (PNG, JPG, or SVG up to 1 MB) — or change/remove an existing one.
Update company name and proprietor line.
Save — the logo and details appear on new printouts.
Coordinate with Branches for per-location address.
settings-organization.example: Ganesan Pawn Shop, Prop.: S. Ganesan, B.A., — shown at top of every pawn receipt printed from the system. When a logo is set, it also appears in the top-left of the sidebar in place of the default badge.
tips: Super Admin only.
Once a logo is uploaded it shows in the sidebar before the app name.
Changes do not reprint old receipts automatically.

settings-branches.title: Branches
settings-branches.purpose: Manage branch codes, addresses, and phone numbers on receipts.
steps: Edit Main branch address and contact numbers.
Add new branches when opening additional shops.
Each branch has separate receipt number sequences.
settings-branches.example: Main — 12 Bazaar Street, Your Town, landline 0XXXX-XXXXXX, mobile 9XXXXXXXXX — appears on printed receipts and SMS.
tips: Use branch selector in toolbar when working multi-branch.
Inactive branches hidden from selectors.

settings-preferences.title: Application Preferences
settings-preferences.purpose: Set receipt print language, cash limit, QR codes, session timeout, dashboard refresh, and optional modules.
steps: Step 1: Open Settings → Application (Super Admin only).
Step 2: Set Dashboard auto-refresh interval (minimum 30 seconds).
Step 3: Set Session timeout — users are logged out after this many minutes of inactivity.
Step 4: Set the cash limit used to flag excess cash at day close.
Step 5: Choose the Receipt print language (Tamil or English) — loan receipts print in this language no matter which language is shown on screen.
Step 6: Toggle Loan item QR codes — when ON, each receipt gets one QR showing customer name, ID, mobile, and receipt number.
Step 7: Enable optional modules — shown on the right: Bank Loans, Auctions, Investments, GL, Notifications.
Step 8: Click Save — sidebar and features update immediately for all users.
Step 9: When you turn QR codes ON, open loans are backfilled automatically.
settings-preferences.example: Set Receipt print language to Tamil while staff use English on screen → loan printouts (labels, sub-category, item, purity, commodity) come out in Tamil, but the app stays in English for the user.
tips: Receipt print language affects printed receipts only, not the on-screen app language.
Optional modules are on the right of the page; the rest of the preferences are on the left.
Disabled modules are hidden from menu and blocked by URL.
QR is one per receipt — not per collateral item.
Super Admin only.

settings-users.title: System Users
settings-users.purpose: Create login accounts for shop staff.
steps: Click Add user with email, name, and role.
Set initial password securely.
User signs in with email and password; can switch UI language.
settings-users.example: cashier@pawn.local, role Cashier — can create loans and part payments but cannot change roles or organization settings.
tips: Use strong passwords in production.
Deactivate users who leave instead of sharing admin login.

settings-roles.title: Roles & Permissions
settings-roles.purpose: Control which modules each role can view, create, edit, or delete.
steps: Select a role from the matrix.
Check permissions per module row.
Save — applies on next login for users with that role.
settings-roles.example: Cashier role: Loans ✓, Renewals ✓, Auctions ✗ — only Manager and Super Admin can open Auction screen.
tips: Super Admin bypasses all checks.
Review after adding new modules.

settings-audit.title: Audit Log
settings-audit.purpose: Timestamped record of sensitive actions for compliance, shown in plain language.
steps: Filter by date range or section (e.g. loans, customers).
Read each row: Date & time (Indian format), User, Action (Created / Updated / Deleted), and the Record affected.
Open the Change details column to see which fields and values were submitted.
Export for audit if required.
settings-audit.example: Filter the Loan section for record #92288 — the row reads 'Updated · Loan #92288' on 14-05-2026 10:32, and Change details shows the new weight, so you can see who changed it and when.
tips: Date & time is shown in Indian standard format (DD-MM-YYYY HH:mm).
Action and Record are friendly labels; hover to see the raw technical value.
Read-only — entries cannot be deleted.
Super Admin and managers typically have access.

masters-categories.title: Commodity Categories
masters-categories.purpose: Top-level pledge types: Gold, Silver, and other commodity classes.
steps: View existing categories (5 imported from legacy).
Add English and Tamil names for new categories.
Deactivate instead of delete if used on old loans.
masters-categories.example: All gold pawns use category Gold (தங்கம்) — interest slabs and tax declarations are configured per category.
tips: Changes affect new loans only.
Sub-categories are configured on the next screen.

masters-sub-categories.title: Sub Categories
masters-sub-categories.purpose: Groupings under each commodity — Ring, Chain, Thali, etc.
steps: Select commodity category first.
Add sub-category with English and Tamil names.
Used when adding collateral lines on new loans.
masters-sub-categories.example: செயின் (Chain) under Gold — when pawning a chain, staff pick Gold → Chain → specific item name on loan form.
tips: 25 sub-categories imported from legacy.
Inactive sub-categories hidden from loan form pickers.

masters-sub-items.title: Sub Items
masters-sub-items.purpose: Specific item names printed on receipts (Tamil/English).
steps: Select category and sub-category.
Add item name — often Tamil as used on legacy receipts.
Pick from cascading dropdown on loan form.
masters-sub-items.example: லேடீஸ்மோதிரம் under Gold → Ring — appears as collateral description on printed receipt.
tips: 185 items imported from legacy pawnshop26.
Duplicate names under same sub-category are prevented.

masters-interest.title: Interest Declarations
masters-interest.purpose: Interest percentage slabs by commodity and loan amount range.
steps: Select commodity (Gold/Silver).
Add row: min amount, max amount, general customer %, other shop %.
Loan form auto-picks rate based on amount and customer type.
masters-interest.example: Gold ₹10,000–₹50,000 at 2.00% per month for general customers — a ₹23,000 loan gets 2.00% automatically.
tips: Other shop rate applies when loan customer type is Other shop.
Overlapping slabs should be avoided.

masters-employees.title: Employees
masters-employees.purpose: Staff master for pay advances and internal reference.
steps: Add employee name, role, and contact.
Use in Pay Advances module.
Deactivate when staff leave.
masters-employees.example: Add employee "Ramesh - Counter" — when recording ₹3,000 advance, select Ramesh from dropdown.
tips: Separate from system login users.
Optional for shops that track advances by name only.
